_comment: Source config for run.py. THREE capture tiers, in order of preference: (1) 'web' = headless capture.py adapters that read a site's own PUBLIC JSON/SSR data with a normal browser UA (no login/CAPTCHA/rate-limit bypass; on any 403/429/challenge we record Source Blocked and stop) — this is the cloud/CI-capable tier; (2) 'apis' = keyless/keyed JSON endpoints via capture_api.py; (3) 'ai_browser' = sources that genuinely need a visible/logged-in browser (anti-bot or login wall) and are captured by the conductor (Codex Chrome plugin / Claude-in-Chrome / Computer Use). Legacy Craigslist RSS lived under 'rss' but 403s even with a browser UA, so Craigslist now runs headless via its own sapi JSON API under 'web'.
_probe_note: Headless reachability probed 2026-06-28 (realistic desktop-Chrome UA): Craigslist sapi JSON 200; Zumper SSR __PRELOADED_STATE__ 200; Reddit JSON 403 (datacenter/UA block — works from a residential IP or with OAuth); Listing Project 200-but-bot-challenge; Furnished Finder 403; Zillow 403 (PerimeterX); Facebook 400/login wall; Kopa search 404 (login-gated). Tier each source by that result.

web.name: Craigslist
web.label: south-bay-rooms
web.note: Rooms/shares near the Santa Clara office — flexible + cheap is top priority.

web.name: Craigslist
web.label: south-bay-sublets
web.note: Sublets/temporary near office — the most-wanted lease shape.

web.name: Craigslist
web.label: south-bay-apartments
web.note: Apartments near office in the solo budget band (newest 50; the band has 4000+).

web.name: Craigslist
web.label: peninsula-rooms
web.note: Caltrain-corridor rooms (San Mateo, Redwood City, Palo Alto).

web.name: Craigslist
web.label: sf-sublets
web.note: SF lifestyle options; Caltrain bike-distance drives SF scoring.

web.name: Zumper
web.label: santa-clara
web.note: Apartment-complex listings (rent ranges + beds) from the page's embedded __PRELOADED_STATE__. Lighter anti-bot than Zillow.

_ai_browser_comment: Sources that need a visible/logged-in browser because they block headless reads (anti-bot or login wall — see _probe_note). The CONDUCTOR captures them (Codex: Chrome plugin; Claude: Claude-in-Chrome / Computer Use), writing ONE JSON array per source into the capture dir as ai-<slug>.json, then re-runs run.py. Capture VISIBLE facts only; never bypass CAPTCHA/login/rate limits; never message posters or submit anything. Facebook Marketplace defaults to the account's saved city -> open the location dialog, type 'Santa Clara, California', pick the City option, Apply; the URL then carries latitude=37.3505&longitude=-121.9427&radius=<km> (use ~24). Cards: a[href*='/marketplace/item/'] -> price/beds-or-title/city lines.

ai_browser.name: Facebook Marketplace (South Bay rentals)
ai_browser.blocked_reason: Login wall (headless returns HTTP 400 / redirects to login). Needs the logged-in session.
ai_browser.note: Logged-in visible browser. If listings show another metro, set location to 'Santa Clara, California'. Subleases/rooms are top priority.

ai_browser.name: Facebook housing groups (Bay Area sublets/rooms)
ai_browser.note: Groups like 'Bay Area Housing, Rooms, Sublets & Roommates'. Capture visible post facts only; do not message posters. Park if the feed is noisy/unreachable.

ai_browser.name: Zillow Rentals (Santa Clara/Sunnyvale, max $4500)
ai_browser.blocked_reason: PerimeterX anti-bot (headless returns HTTP 403). No keyless JSON path.
ai_browser.note: Apply price filter in the UI; capture rent, beds, lease, address, url. If it challenges, record Source Blocked and move on.

ai_browser.name: Furnished Finder (Santa Clara area)
ai_browser.blocked_reason: Anti-bot (headless returns HTTP 403 even with full browser headers).
ai_browser.note: Monthly furnished; flexible terms — strong for early-career movers.

ai_browser.name: The Listing Project
ai_browser.blocked_reason: Bot challenge interstitial on headless fetch (200 but no real listings).
ai_browser.note: Curated SF/Bay sublets and shares; may also require an email subscription to view.

ai_browser.name: Kopa furnished sublets (near SCU/Stanford)
ai_browser.blocked_reason: No public search route (every /search|/listings path 404s headlessly); search is login-gated.
ai_browser.note: Furnished short-term sublets. Sign in, search Santa Clara, capture visible cards. Park if it requires account actions.

_apis_comment: Free/keyed JSON endpoints fetched by capture_api.py. No-key entries (e.g. Reddit) are best-effort; keyed entries skip until their key_env is set. {city} in url is substituted from each 'cities' value. We never add proxy/CAPTCHA/stealth code.

apis.name: Reddit
apis.note: Public Reddit JSON. Probed 2026-06-28: returns HTTP 403 to plain UAs from datacenter IPs (no UA variant helped). Works from a residential IP (the LOCAL scheduled run) or with a free Reddit OAuth app token. Until then it records Source Blocked in cloud/CI runs. No login/key required for the local path.

apis.name: RentCast
apis.note: Optional, OFF by default. Legit rentals/condos API with a free tier (~50 calls/mo). Set RENTCAST_API_KEY + enabled:true to use; listings have no public URL so they land in Needs Verification.
